summary:
- Keep original search order but apply a stable sort that prioritizes: (1) titles containing all query tokens (after simple punctuation normalization) and (2) titles containing the exact query phrase.
- No aggressive rewrites or heavy rescoring; minimal, safe reordering to prefer strong lexical matches in titles.
- Improves relevance for queries where exact terms appear in the product title (e.g., brand/model/phrase-specific searches) while avoiding broader ranking changes.
message: Updated reranker to stably promote documents whose title contains all query tokens and/or the exact query phrase. This conservative, lexical key boosts strong matches without broadly reordering results. Mean NDCG improved on evals and passed validation thresholds.

summary: Reranker now ranks by: (1) phrase present in title, (2) full token coverage over title+description, (3) token count in title, (4) phrase present in description, and (5) earlier phrase position in title. This boosted mean NDCG on validation from 0.5971 to 0.6017.
message: Updated reranker to improve relevance. Key changes: it now considers both title and description, prioritizes exact phrase matches in the title, ensures full token coverage across title+description, uses token match counts as tie-breakers, and favors earlier phrase positions in titles. Validation NDCG improved from 0.5971 to 0.6017.

summary:
- Pull 20 candidates instead of 10 for stronger re-ranking headroom.
- Use query_rewrite to build variant list; reward documents that contain all tokens of at least one variant.
- Keep strong exact title signals (startswith, contains) and original query position tie-breaker.
- Validation NDCG improved beyond threshold; training mean NDCG rose to 0.6040.
message: Reranker improved and validated. I replaced the function to: 1) increase the candidate pool (top_k=20), and 2) leverage spelling/acronym variants from query_rewrite. The new scoring prioritizes: title startswith → title contains → all tokens of at least one variant are present → token count in title → variant in description → position of original query. This yielded a validated NDCG improvement ≥ 0.001 and increased training mean NDCG from 0.6017 to 0.6040.

summary: Multiple small heuristic improvements showed minor NDCG gains but failed to exceed the +0.001 validation threshold, so the reranker was left unchanged to avoid regressions or overfitting.
message: I attempted multiple targeted edits to the reranker to improve validation NDCG (>= +0.001), including: leveraging rewrite phrase signals, combined title+description phrase checks, improved tokenization (handling sizes with 'x', apostrophes, ampersands), token overlap adjustments, and ordering tweaks in the sort key. While a few combinations yielded small gains (up to about +0.000874), none consistently surpassed the required +0.001 improvement threshold on the validation set. Since no patch met the acceptance guardrails, I did not apply any changes and left the original reranker code intact.

summary: Tried several compact edits: (1) add rewrite-aware title/any-text boosts, (2) expand token set using rewrites, (3) minor key ordering/tie-breakers. Best validation delta was ~+0.00055, below the +0.001 requirement, so all patches were rejected. Reranker remains as-is.
message: No code changes were applied. I explored multiple small, generalizable tweaks (e.g., leveraging query rewrites in title/any-text checks, expanding token coverage using rewrite variants, adjusting feature order, and adding light tie-breakers). While a few variants yielded slight NDCG gains on validation (up to ~+0.00055), none met the required +0.001 improvement threshold; those patches were therefore rejected by the guardrails. I’ve left the reranker unchanged to avoid regressions.
